meta.description: Ride Southeast Asia's first bullet train at 350 km/h from Bandung to Jakarta in just 34 minutes. Includes White Crater volcano, factory outlets, Taman Mini, and Monas. Private tour from RM1388. Book with Dhesu Travel.
meta.keywords: WHOOSH bullet train tour Malaysia, Jakarta Bandung high speed train, Kereta Cepat Indonesia, White Crater Bandung tour, Indonesia bullet train, Jakarta tour package from KL

hero.title: 4 Days Jakarta Bandung
hero.intro: Ride Southeast Asia's first and only high-speed bullet train — covering 143 km from Bandung to Jakarta in just 34 minutes at speeds of 350 km/h. Combine this historic rail experience with Bandung's volcanic White Crater, iconic factory outlets, and Jakarta's cultural landmarks in one exciting 4-day private tour.

breadcrumb.label: 4 Days Jakarta Bandung WHOOSH Bullet Train

hero.heroImageAlt: WHOOSH high-speed bullet train at Padalarang station West Java Indonesia

includes: Hotel accommodation (3 nights: 2 nights Bandung, 1 night Jakarta)
All meals as specified (Day 1: Lunch & Dinner; Days 2 & 3: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner; Day 4: Breakfast)
WHOOSH high-speed bullet train tickets (Padalarang to Halim)

overview.heading: Factory Outlets, Volcanoes & Southeast Asia's Only Bullet Train
body: The WHOOSH (Whoosh) — Indonesia's Kereta Cepat Jakarta Bandung — opened in 2023 as Southeast Asia's first high-speed railway, connecting Bandung and Jakarta at speeds up to 350 km/h. A journey that once took 3 hours by road now takes just 34 minutes by rail. This tour builds an unforgettable Bandung-Jakarta experience around that train ride: two days exploring Bandung's volcanic White Crater, famous leather goods district, and factory outlets, then a dramatic bullet train crossing to Jakarta for cultural sightseeing.

The itinerary is virtually identical to our White Crater Volcano package — with one critical upgrade. Instead of a road transfer from Bandung to Jakarta, you board the WHOOSH at Padalarang Station and arrive at Halim Station in Jakarta 34 minutes later, having crossed West Java's hills and valleys at a speed no road can match. All meals, entrance fees, the bullet train tickets, and your private guide are included from arrival at Jakarta airport to final departure.

items.title: WHOOSH Bullet Train — 350 km/h Across West Java
items.body: Board the WHOOSH at Padalarang Station — a modern, airy terminal west of Bandung — and settle in for a 34-minute journey to Jakarta's Halim Station. The train reaches 350 km/h as it crosses the West Java countryside, making this the fastest ground travel experience in Southeast Asia. A genuine once-in-a-lifetime transport milestone.
items.imageAlt: WHOOSH high-speed train on the Jakarta-Bandung high-speed rail line

items.title: Kawah Putih White Crater Volcano
items.body: A turquoise-white sulphuric crater lake formed by an 11th-century eruption of Mount Patuha, Kawah Putih is one of Java's most ethereal natural attractions. The colour of the water changes throughout the day and the cool highland air at 2,430 metres makes it a refreshing contrast to Bandung's city heat.
items.imageAlt: Kawah Putih White Crater volcanic lake turquoise sulphuric water Bandung

items.title: Rengganis Suspension Bridge
items.body: Southeast Asia's longest suspension bridge — a thrilling walk across a highland valley near the White Crater area, offering panoramic views of volcanic peaks, tea plantations, and Bandung's surrounding hills. An adrenaline highlight for those comfortable with heights.
items.imageAlt: Rengganis suspension bridge over Bandung highland valley

items.title: Bandung Shopping & Jakarta Culture
items.body: Bandung's Riau factory outlets and Cibaduyut leather goods district offer Malaysian shoppers exceptional value, while Jakarta's Taman Mini Indonesia Indah and the National Monument (Monas) provide Indonesia's most comprehensive cultural experience — all covered in a single 4-day itinerary.
items.imageAlt: National Monument Monas Jakarta with Taman Mini Indonesia display

days.title: Arrive Jakarta > Bandung > Riau Factory Outlets
days.description: Your guide meets you at Soekarno-Hatta International Airport, Jakarta, and transfers you to Bandung — approximately 3 hours via the Cipularang Toll Road. Stop for lunch at a local restaurant en route. Arrive in Bandung, West Java's highland fashion capital at 768 metres above sea level. Head straight to the famous Riau factory outlet district — multiple outlet stores spread across the Riau-Setiabudi area offering branded and locally made fashion, embroidery, bags, and accessories at factory prices. A long-standing destination for Malaysian shoppers seeking quality clothing at excellent prices. Dinner at a local Bandung restaurant and overnight in the city.
suggestedActivities: Shop the Riau factory outlet district
Browse embroidered fashion and Bandung clothing brands
Explore Bandung's vibrant food scene near the hotel

days.title: Cibaduyut Leather > White Crater Volcano > Rengganis Bridge
days.description: After hotel breakfast, drive to Cibaduyut — Bandung's famous leather goods street, lined with workshops and showrooms producing and selling shoes, bags, wallets, belts, and jackets at prices far below retail. Browse the family-run stores and negotiate for the best deals on genuine leather products. After lunch, drive south into the volcanic highlands to Kawah Putih (White Crater) — one of Java's most photogenic natural attractions. The turquoise-white crater lake of Mount Patuha sits at 2,430 metres elevation, its colour shifting from jade green to milky white depending on sulphur concentration and time of day. The surrounding highland air is cool and fresh. Nearby, walk the Rengganis Suspension Bridge for sweeping views across the volcanic highland valley — one of West Java's most dramatic and photogenic viewpoints. Clubbing Lake Side offers additional vantage points over the crater complex. Dinner and overnight in Bandung.
suggestedActivities: Shop leather shoes and bags at Cibaduyut district
Photograph Kawah Putih's colour-shifting crater lake
Cool highland air and volcanic scenery

days.title: Bandung > WHOOSH Bullet Train > Jakarta > Taman Mini > Monas
days.description: After hotel breakfast and checkout, transfer to WHOOSH Station Padalarang — a sleek modern terminal that is the Bandung gateway to Indonesia's high-speed rail network. Board the WHOOSH (Kereta Cepat Jakarta Bandung) for one of Southeast Asia's most exciting transport experiences: 143 kilometres from Bandung to Jakarta covered in just 34 minutes at a top speed of 350 km/h. Arrive at Halim Station in East Jakarta and be collected by your guide. Lunch at a local Jakarta restaurant, then spend the afternoon exploring Indonesia's capital. Taman Mini Indonesia Indah is a vast cultural park where full-scale traditional houses from all 34 Indonesian provinces are displayed alongside cultural museums, craft workshops, and regular performing arts — the best single overview of Indonesia's extraordinary cultural diversity. Continue to Merdeka Square for the National Monument (Monas) — a 132-metre gold-tipped obelisk symbolising Indonesian independence since 1975, one of Southeast Asia's most iconic landmarks. Dinner and overnight in Jakarta.
suggestedActivities: Ride the WHOOSH bullet train at 350 km/h
Explore Taman Mini Indonesia Indah cultural park
Photograph Monas National Monument at golden hour
Dinner at a Jakarta signature restaurant

days.title: Jakarta Shopping > Departure
days.description: A final hotel breakfast and checkout before a last burst of Jakarta shopping. Choose between Thamrin City Mall — a multi-storey bazaar specialising in batik, fashion, traditional crafts, and souvenirs — or Mangga Dua Square, Jakarta's hub for electronics, wholesale fashion, and accessories. Both are excellent for gifts and last-minute purchases. Your guide transfers you to Soekarno-Hatta International Airport in good time for your departure flight back to Malaysia. End of tour.
suggestedActivities: Last shopping at Thamrin City or Mangga Dua

whyBook.heading: Why Book Through Dhesu
whyBook.body: Dhesu Travel has been organising private Jakarta and Bandung tours for Malaysian travellers since 1988. We were among the first Malaysian agencies to incorporate the WHOOSH bullet train into our itineraries, and our ground team knows the Padalarang station procedures, boarding process, and optimal timing to make the most of this historic rail journey.

points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency, regulated and insured for your peace of mind.

points.body: WHOOSH train tickets from Padalarang to Halim are fully included in the package price — no extras.

sections.heading: WHOOSH Bullet Train Tour from Malaysia — 4 Days Jakarta Bandung
sections.body: The WHOOSH (Kereta Cepat Jakarta Bandung) opened in October 2023 and immediately became one of Southeast Asia's most talked-about transport experiences. Travelling at up to 350 km/h, it covers the 143 km between Bandung's Padalarang Station and Jakarta's Halim Station in just 34 minutes — a journey that previously took 3 hours by road. This 4-day private tour builds on our popular White Crater Volcano package by replacing the road transfer with the bullet train, adding a genuinely memorable transit experience. Malaysian passport holders need no visa for Indonesia, and direct flights from KL to Jakarta (CGK) take under 2 hours. Starting from RM1,388 per person with all meals, transport, entrance fees, and bullet train tickets included.

sections.heading: Is the WHOOSH Worth It? What Malaysian Travellers Say
sections.body: For Malaysian travellers accustomed to the ETS or KTM network, the WHOOSH is a revelation. The train is smooth, quiet, and extremely fast — 350 km/h feels comfortable inside the air-conditioned carriages. The stations at Padalarang and Halim are modern and well-organised, and boarding is straightforward with some prior guidance from your Dhesu guide. The 34-minute journey itself becomes a highlight of the trip rather than a transfer — many guests say it was the most talked-about part of their Bandung-Jakarta experience. If you're considering the standard road transfer versus the bullet train, the upgrade to RM1,388 (vs RM1,288) is well worth it for the experience alone.

items.question: Do I need a visa to visit Indonesia?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders can enter Indonesia visa-free for stays up to 30 days. Your passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your travel dates.

items.question: What is the WHOOSH bullet train and how fast is it?
items.answer: WHOOSH (Whoosh) is Indonesia's Kereta Cepat Jakarta Bandung — Southeast Asia's first high-speed railway, opened in October 2023. It operates at a maximum speed of 350 km/h and covers the 143 km between Padalarang (near Bandung) and Halim (Jakarta) in approximately 34 minutes. It is operated by a joint venture of Indonesian and Chinese railway companies.

items.question: Is the WHOOSH bullet train included in the package price?
items.answer: Yes. WHOOSH bullet train tickets from Padalarang Station to Halim Station are fully included in the package price. Your guide accompanies you to the station, assists with boarding, and your Jakarta guide collects you at Halim Station.

items.question: How does this package compare to the standard White Crater Volcano tour?
items.answer: The itinerary is almost identical — both cover Kawah Putih White Crater, Cibaduyut leather goods, Riau factory outlets, Taman Mini, and Monas. The key difference is Day 3: this package uses the WHOOSH bullet train to travel from Bandung to Jakarta (34 minutes, 350 km/h) instead of the standard road transfer (3 hours). The bullet train package costs RM100 more per person but delivers a genuinely historic and thrilling transit experience.

items.question: Which airport do I fly into for this tour?
items.answer: You fly into Soekarno-Hatta International Airport (CGK) in Jakarta. Direct flights from Kuala Lumpur (KUL) are available on AirAsia, Malaysia Airlines, Batik Air, and Malindo Air — flight time is under 2 hours. The tour begins and ends at Soekarno-Hatta.
